ogTitle: Blackheath vs Blackmans Point: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Blackheath and Blackmans Point.
heading: Blackheath vs Blackmans Point — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Blackheath, New South Wales and Blackmans Point, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Blackheath has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Blackmans Point (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Blackheath or Blackmans Point safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Blackheath has a flood risk score of 49/100, while Blackmans Point scores 41/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Blackheath scores 46/100 for bushfire risk. Blackmans Point scores 26/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Blackheath or Blackmans Point?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Blackheath has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.4% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
